subject: Databases
title: SQL Dialects
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on SQL Dialects.

hook.text: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle — they all speak SQL, but each adds its own twist.

buildup.text: The ANSI SQL standard defines the core language. Most databases follow it, then add extensions.

discovery.text: Postgres has JSONB operators. MySQL has LIMIT. SQL Server has TOP. Same idea, different syntax.

twist.text: Write standard SQL and your queries work almost everywhere. Use extensions and you're locked in.

climax.text: Knowing standard SQL transfers across every database. Dialect details are easy to pick up.

punchline.text: Learn standard SQL first. Dialects are small detours.

quiz.question: What is the relationship between ANSI SQL and database dialects?
options: ANSI SQL defines the core standard; each database adds its own extensions
ANSI SQL only works with Oracle databases
